title: Expose borrowed iteration with iter
scenario: Callers often need to inspect a collection wrapper without consuming it. A borrowed iterator exposes a view without leaking fields.
instructions: The starter already has OrderLines::new, len, and is_empty. Add OrderLines::iter(&self) -> std::slice::Iter<'_, OrderLine>.

hints.body: Add iter to the existing OrderLines impl block.
Return self.lines.iter() so callers can inspect lines without consuming the wrapper.
One complete solution for Expose borrowed iteration with iter is shown below.

completionExplanation: Borrowed iteration exposes read-only access to the wrapped lines without making the Vec field public.
